Orchid Island Capital, Inc. 6M 2026: Revenue $322M, EPS $0.35— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Orchid Island Capital, Inc. reported results for the six months ended June 30, 2026, with higher interest income and a return to profitability versus the prior-year period. Interest income rose sharply, helping the REIT deliver net income attributable to common stockholders of $69.2M and diluted EPS of $0.35 for the six-month period.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $322.0M interest income for six months ended June 30, 2026, compared with $173.4M for six months ended June 30, 2025; YoY change 85.6%.
  • Net income: $69.2M net income attributable to common stockholders for six months ended June 30, 2026, compared with a $(16.5M) loss in the six months ended June 30, 2025.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.35 diluted EPS for six months ended June 30, 2026, compared with $(0.16) for the six months ended June 30, 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Portfolio Growth: Agency RMBS holdings grew to $11.5B, with a larger average portfolio size and higher yields driving increased interest income.
  • Funding & Hedging Shift: Management expanded its hedging mix — using swaps, futures and options — to manage short-term repo funding cost volatility and interest-rate risk.
  • Capital Raising & Equity Issuances: Executed multiple ATM equity distribution programs, raising proceeds to support portfolio deployment.
  • Operational Scaling: Expanded repurchase agreement counterparties and maintained broad repo facilities with stable operational margining and daily collateral management.
  • Dividend & Distribution Focus: Continued regular monthly dividends and declared additional dividends to support REIT qualification and shareholder income.

Original SEC Filing: Orchid Island Capital, Inc. [ ORC ] - 10-Q - Jul. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: CME Group 2Q 2026: Revenue $1.71B, EPS $2.88— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
CME Group reported second-quarter 2026 results with revenue of $1.706B and diluted EPS of $2.88, modestly above year-ago levels as electronic trading volumes and market data strength offset mixed interest-rate activity.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $1.706B for Q2 2026, up from $1.692B in the year-ago quarter; YoY change 1%.
  • Net income: $1.042B for Q2 2026, versus $1.025B in the year-ago quarter; YoY change 2%.
  • Diluted EPS: $2.88 for Q2 2026, up from $2.81 in the year-ago quarter; YoY change 2%.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth was modest for the quarter, with six-month revenue up about 8% driven by higher market data and information services revenue year-to-date.
  • Electronic trading remained dominant at 94% of activity; CME Globex volumes rose while open outcry declined.
  • Product mix saw volume increases in equity indexes, metals, energy and crypto, while interest rate volumes were mixed.
  • Market data & information services revenue showed momentum, up 18% year-to-date driven by higher usage, new customers and price increases.
  • Operational initiatives continued, including the Google Cloud transformation and elevated technology spending to modernize the platform.

Original SEC Filing: CME GROUP INC. [ CME ] - 10-Q - Jul. 24, 2026

Disclaimer

Scribe Therapeutics Soars 67% After $128.7 Million Upsized IPO
data.description:
Scribe Therapeutics , a clinical-stage developer of gene therapies for heart disease, surged 67% on Friday after raising $128.7 million in an upsized initial public offering. The Alameda, California-based biotechnology company opened at $25 per share in New York, significantly above its $15 IPO price. Scribe sold 8.58 million shares on Thursday after initially marketing 7.15 million shares at between $13 and $15 each, while the first-day trading level gave the company a market value of approximately $440.3 million based on the outstanding shares disclosed in its filings.

The offering also attracted interest from established pharmaceutical investors. Sanofi SA, a pharmaceutical company participating through a concurrent private placement, agreed through its affiliates to purchase approximately $7.5 million of Scribe shares at the IPO price. Eli Lilly & Co. , one of Scribe's existing backers, indicated that it was interested in purchasing enough shares to own as much as 11% of the company following the IPO and private placement. Entities affiliated with Andreessen Horowitz and Avoro Life Sciences Fund were also listed among Scribe's largest shareholders, suggesting that the company entered the public market with support from several prominent investment and pharmaceutical groups.

Scribe is evaluating gene-editing technologies for heart and metabolic diseases, with its initial work focused on atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease. The company expects to report initial data during the first half of 2027 from an Australian trial involving adults with an increased risk of the disease, which investors may view as an important future clinical milestone. However, Scribe remains loss-making, reporting a net loss of $17.4 million on collaboration revenue of $2.2 million for the three months ended March 31, compared with a $3.5 million loss on revenue of $17.1 million during the same period a year earlier. Leerink Partners, Goldman Sachs Group Inc., Guggenheim Securities and Wells Fargo & Co., financial institutions involved in capital markets and securities services, led the IPO.

data.title: Cariboo Rose Begins Drilling at Lightning Strike Gold Silver Project
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

Vancouver, BC, July 24th, 2026 – TheNewswire – Cariboo Rose Resources Ltd. (TSX-V: CRB) Cariboo Rose Resources Ltd. (TSX: CRB) “Cariboo Rose” is pleased to report that drilling has started on its 100% owned, 4,193-hectare (10,357 acre), Lightning Strike Gold Silver Project located 60 kilometers northeast of the community of 100 Mile House, British Columbia. In 2025 two diamond drill holes were completed on this project with one of the holes, LSD25-01, cutting 44.4 meters grading 0.77 g/t gold and 37.1 g/t silver including 11.9 meters grading 1.58 g/t gold and 109.7 g/t silver. The current program anticipates at least five new drill holes.

Lightning Strike is host to orogenic sediment hosted gold‐silver mineralization and in a geological setting similar to the Spanish Mountain and Frasergold (FG) occurrences located 110 kilometers and 40 kilometers north, respectively. It is situated on the eastern edge of the Quesnel Terrane to the west of the Eureka Thrust. The property is dominantly underlain by argillaceous Nicola Group sediments with Nicola volcanic rocks (Mesozoic). It is conceptually analogous to the giant Asian deposits of Muruntau and Sukhoi Log.

Historical work at Lightning Strike by Homestake Mining in 1984 and later by Skygold Resources ltd. (now Spanish Mountain Gold Corp.) in 2008 to 2012 discovered an area of gold and silver mineralization in the western part of the current claim block. Trenching here uncovered quartz‐carbonate boulders with gold values to 25.1 g/t gold and silver values to 216.8 g/t silver. The target was drilled from 2008 to 2010 with 31 diamond drill holes completed. Almost all of the drill holes returned at least one anomalous to strongly mineralized gold or gold silver intercept. Cariboo Rose first staked and conducted work on the claims in 2019, carrying out geological reconnaissance, soil sampling, claim expansion, two reverse circulation drill programs and one diamond drill program since that time.

Significant historical drill intercepts at Lightning Strike include 0.77 g/t gold over 54.0 meters, 3.42 g/t gold and 228.1g/t silver over 5.0 meters, 2.88 g/t gold over 24.0 meters and 8.84 g/t gold over 7.5 meters.

J.W. (Bill) Morton, P. Geo., a Director of Cariboo Rose Resources Ltd. and a Qualified Person under National Instrument 43-101, has reviewed and approved this news release.

Contact:

(604) 681-7913

Toll Free: 888-656-6611

www.cariboorose.com

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Copyright (c) 2026 TheNewswire - All rights reserved.

data.title: Tesla Avoids Door Safety Probe, But New Rules Could Follow
data.description:
Tesla avoided a formal federal safety investigation into its door-release design, but regulators said the complaints exposed a broader gap in existing vehicle rules.

The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration denied a petition seeking an investigation into Tesla's mechanical door releases after reviewing the available evidence. The complaints followed several crashes in which occupants allegedly struggled to open vehicle doors before suffering additional fire-related injuries.

NHTSA said the issue is better handled through new industrywide regulations because current federal rules do not clearly govern the placement or labeling of internal manual door releases. The agency acknowledged that a concealed or difficult-to-find release could prevent occupants from escaping during a crash or fire.

That means Tesla does not face an immediate recall tied to the petition. However, future standards could require automakers to make emergency door releases easier to locate and operate across all vehicles.

Richard Myerscough Sells Securities In ALUULA Composites Inc.
data.description:
VICTORIA, BC, July 24, 2026 /CNW/ -- On July 22, 2026, Richard Myerscough, through Winston Ventures Inc. ("Winston") and 1485963 B.C. Ltd. ("148", and together with Winston, the "Affiliated Entities"), each of which are controlled by Mr. Myerscough, completed a private sale of 645,000 common shares ("Common Shares") in the authorized share structure of ALUULA Composites Inc. () (the "Company") to an arm's length party at a price of C$3.30 per Common Share for gross aggregate proceeds of C$2,128,500.00 (the "Transaction").

The Transaction was completed through an orderly block sale following continued institutional interest in the Company. The block sale accommodated a portion of this institutional demand without prolonged selling activity in the public market.

Prior to completion of the Transaction, Mr. Myerscough beneficially owned or exercised control or direction over, directly or indirectly, jointly with the Affiliated Entities, 5,645,256 Common Shares of the Company, representing approximately 18.27% of the issued and outstanding Common Shares of the Company.

Upon completion of the Transaction, Mr. Myerscough and the Affiliated Entities have control and direction over 5,000,256 Common Shares, representing approximately 16.18% of the issued and outstanding Common Shares of the Company. Mr. Myerscough, through the Affiliated Entities, remains the Company's single largest shareholder. He will continue to serve on the Board of Directors of the Company, with the substantial majority of his investment remaining in the Company, ensuring his interests continue to be closely aligned with those of all shareholders.

Mr. Myerscough sold Common Shares as part of his prudent long-term retirement and financial planning. Mr. Myerscough has no current or future plans for purchases or sales of Common Shares at this time; however, he may acquire or dispose securities of the Company depending on market conditions, changes in plans or other relevant factors, subject in each case to applicable securities laws.

This news release is being issued as required by National Instrument 62-103 - The Early Warning System and Related Take-Over Bid and Insider Reporting Issues. An Early Warning Report will be filed in connection with the Transactions on the System for Electronic Document Analysis and Retrieval Plus (SEDAR+) under the Company's profile at www.sedarplus.ca.

For further information or to request a copy of the Early Warning Report to which this press release relates, please contact Richard Myerscough at 250-744-9467. The Company's head office is located at #300, 4240 Glanford Avenue, Victoria, British Columbia V8Z 4B8, Canada.

THE TSX VENTURE EXCHANGE HAS NOT REVIEWED AND DOES NOT ACCEPT RESPONSIBILITY FOR THE ACCURACY OF THIS RELEASE.

SOURCE Richard Myerscough

data.title: BV Financial reports Q2 net income $3.5M; adjusted diluted EPS $0.47
data.description:
BV Financial reported net income of $3.5 million, or $0.42 per diluted share, for the quarter ended June 30, 2026, up from $2.9 million, or $0.29 per diluted share, in Q2 2025. Adjusted net income was $3.9 million, or $0.47 per diluted share, for Q2 2026; operating pre-provision net revenue (OPPNR) was $5.2 million, or $0.63 per diluted share. For the six-month period, net income was $4.6 million ($0.55 diluted) and adjusted net income was $7.1 million ($0.86 diluted).

Financial Highlights

  • Net income (GAAP) for Q2 2026: $3.5 million; diluted EPS: $0.42 (Q2 2025: $2.9 million, $0.29 diluted).
  • Adjusted net income (Non-GAAP) for Q2 2026: $3.9 million; adjusted diluted EPS: $0.47 (Q2 2025 adjusted diluted EPS: $0.37).
  • Operating Pre-Provision Net Revenue (OPPNR) for Q2 2026: $5.2 million; OPPNR per diluted share: $0.63 (Q2 2025 OPPNR: $5.4M, $0.54 diluted).
  • Net interest income for Q2 2026: $9.5 million; net interest margin: 4.58% (Q2 2025: $9.2M; NIM 4.36%).
  • Six months ended June 30, 2026 — net income: $4.6 million ($0.55 diluted); adjusted net income: $7.1 million ($0.86 diluted); OPPNR YTD: $10.1 million ($1.22 diluted).

Business Highlights

  • Total assets were $877.9 million at June 30, 2026, down $34.3 million from December 31, 2025, primarily due to using cash from loan runoff to repay borrowings.
  • Net loans decreased $44.3 million (5.9%) to $710.6 million versus December 31, 2025; largest reductions in construction & land, commercial investor real estate and commercial loan categories.
  • All $35.0 million of outstanding Federal Home Loan Bank of Atlanta borrowings at March 31, 2026 were repaid during Q2, reducing interest expense and FHLB stock requirements.
  • Deposits held essentially steady at $675.9 million at June 30, 2026 versus $676.1 million at year-end; interest-bearing deposits were flat at $138.4 million.
  • Asset quality: non-performing loans increased to $3.4 million at June 30, 2026 from $2.3 million at December 31, 2025; allowance for credit losses on loans was $6.2 million (0.87% of total loans).

Original SEC Filing: BV Financial, Inc. [ BVFL ] - 8-K - Jul. 24, 2026

Disclaimer

ZVRA Stock Sinks 23% On EU Setback — Analyst Says Risk Is ‘A Headwind, But Not Insurmountable’
data.description:
Key points:
  • Zevra plans to request a re-examination and will submit a detailed response.
  • H.C. Wainwright called the negative opinion on Zevra Therapeutics’ marketing application for arimoclomol “a disappointing outcome that clearly increases the regulatory risk around the European opportunity.”
  • Arimoclomol is already approved and marketed in the U.S. under the brand name MIPLYFFA.

Shares of Zevra Therapeutics (ZVRA) fell by as much as 27% on Friday but pared some losses after H.C. Wainwright noted that the company’s recent setback for its drug arimoclomol in Europe was “not insurmountable.”

At the time of writing, the stock was down 23%, on track for its worst day since March 2021.

ZVRA’s Setback

On Friday, Zevra announced that the EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) adopted a negative opinion on its application to market arimoclomol for Niemann-Pick disease type C (NPC), a rare progressive neurodegenerative disorder.

The committee concluded that the drug’s effectiveness had not been clearly proven. It cited uncertainties in how the trial data were handled and analyzed, and noted no clear benefit in walking ability or cognition. Although patients already taking miglustat, alongside arimoclomol, showed some positive signs, these were not considered robust enough for the overall population. As a result, the benefits did not outweigh the risks, the committee said.

Zevra plans to request a re-examination and will submit a detailed response. CEO Neil F. McFarlane said the company remains “confident in the totality of evidence” and is committed to finding pathways to bring the treatment to European patients.

Analyst Weighs In

H.C. Wainwright called the negative opinion on Zevra Therapeutics’ marketing application for arimoclomol “a disappointing outcome that clearly increases the regulatory risk around the European opportunity.” The firm maintained its ‘Buy’ rating and $26 price target, noting that Zevra plans to request a re-examination and that this is not the end of the process. Analysts left estimates and valuation unchanged ahead of the company’s August 5 earnings call.

Arimoclomol is already approved and marketed in the U.S. under the brand name MIPLYFFA. The FDA cleared it in September 2024 as the first treatment for neurological symptoms of NPC in patients aged 2 years and older, used in combination with miglustat. MIPLYFFA generated $87.4 million in net revenue for the full year 2025 and accounted for a lion’s share of the company's total net revenue of $106.5 million.

How Did ZVRA Retail Traders React?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around ZVRA stock stayed within the ‘extremely bullish’ territory over the past 24 hours, while message volume rose from ‘high’ to ‘extremely high’ levels.

A Stocktwits user noted that Zevra has not provided details pertaining to the timeline for its further submission.

Another opined that Zevra’s success may come down to whether its drug is superior to privately held pharma company IntraBio’s Aquneursa, also approved for Niemann-Pick disease Type C.

ZVRA stock has gained 8% year-to-date.
